Container.Name: CacheDeck
Container.Overview: Persistent SteamPrefill dashboard with a visual game library, update queue, live progress, scheduling information and diagnostics.
Container.Description: CacheDeck provides a browser interface for SteamPrefill and LANCache. Select games, browse a visual Steam library, queue one-click game updates, launch detached prefill jobs, reconnect to live output, review run history, inspect the configured schedule and collect diagnostics without opening an Unraid terminal. Prefills continue after the browser disconnects.
Container.Requires: An existing SteamPrefill container. CacheDeck requires access to the Docker socket and should remain accessible only on trusted networks.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port used to open the CacheDeck web interface.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Required so CacheDeck can inspect and execute commands in SteamPrefill.

Config.Name: Persistent Config
Config.Description: Persistent CacheDeck history, game library metadata and per-game update queue.

Config.Name: SteamPrefill Container
Config.Description: Exact name of the existing SteamPrefill container.

Config.Name: SteamPrefill Directory
Config.Description: SteamPrefill installation directory inside the target container.

Config.Name: SteamPrefill User
Config.Description: Linux user CacheDeck should use inside the target container.

Config.Name: Target Job State Directory
Config.Description: Writable directory inside SteamPrefill used for the active detached job and live output.

Config.Name: Automatic Resume
Config.Description: When true, CacheDeck makes one automatic restart attempt after a managed prefill is interrupted by a target-container restart.

Config.Name: History Limit
Config.Description: Number of completed or detected runs retained in /config.
